jurisdiction_and_domain: United Kingdom occupational health and safety guidance for arboriculture and work at height

pages.title: Lifting equipment in arboriculture
pages.treatment: planning, supervision, system boundary, person-as-load, tree anchor as component, species-age-condition-disease context, movement hazards, daily checks, written rope inspection, thorough examination, and rescue retained with operational and legal exclusions

pages.title: Aerial work: Overview
pages.treatment: ground-team planning, staffing, rescue availability, communication, observation, line housekeeping, public exclusion, workload, and stop-and-reassess rule retained; qualifications and equipment links excluded

pages.title: Tree climbing
pages.treatment: task-specific planning and training, rescue, anchor roles, backup concepts, separate working and safety lines, and supplementary-anchor context retained; configurations, techniques, devices, exceptions, video, industry guides, and PDFs excluded

pages.title: Lifting Operations and Lifting Equipment Regulations (LOLER)
pages.treatment: current HSE routing relationship verified; navigation and link-title content excluded from training

pages.title: Thorough examinations and inspections of lifting equipment
pages.treatment: inspection layers, deterioration, exceptional events, competent-person judgment, impartiality, records, dangerous-defect communication, and withdrawal retained; exact intervals, testing, enforcement, standards, and legal fields excluded

pages.title: Working at height: Overview
pages.treatment: planning, organisation, supervision, competence, hierarchy, suitable equipment, inspection, maintenance, emergency planning, and rescue retained; statistics, cases, devices, and legal links excluded

pages.title: Inspecting fall arrest equipment made from webbing or rope
pages.treatment: only the landing-page statement that generic fall-arrest inspection principles may apply to similar rope equipment was retained; linked PDF excluded and not fetched

pages.title: Copyright
pages.treatment: rights provenance only; contact details, print-product exclusions, and other legal boilerplate excluded

rights_review.hse_statement_scope: HSE says website information is Crown-owned and Crown-copyright-protected unless otherwise indicated, and Crown material may be reused under the OGL.
rights_review.preferred_acknowledgement: Contains public sector information published by the Health and Safety Executive and licensed under the Open Government Licence.
rights_review.component_warning: HSE states that some images, illustrations, and multimedia may not be Crown-owned and that the HSE logo requires prior written permission.
rights_review.included_treatment: Original paraphrase and synthesis of eligible substantive Crown text with page-level source labels, UK occupational scope, and change notice.
excluded_components: header, footer, breadcrumbs, navigation, search, and site chrome
all images, illustrations, videos, and multimedia
external training video and embedded-player destinations
Arboricultural Association pages and learning products
copyright contact details and legal boilerplate

component_and_link_audit.hse_logo_location: site header outside substantive page content
linked_pdf_routes_found: HSE treework research and chainsaw publications
third_party_link_classes_found: Arboricultural Association industry code and learning
component_and_link_audit.audit_note: Page chrome and linked components were enumerated and excluded. No content image, logo, SVG, video, PDF, or third-party page is part of the direct-training artifact.

normalization_and_review_method.normalized_main_text_method: For audit metrics only: Python standard-library HTMLParser entered main#contentContainer, excluded aside, nav, script, and style, captured h1-h4, p, and li text, collapsed internal whitespace, wrote one nonempty captured element per LF-terminated line, and hashed UTF-8 bytes.
normalization_and_review_method.automation_boundary: HTML parsing supported navigation, link and media enumeration, headings, page-level metrics, and claim verification. The training Markdown was manually selected, rewritten, reorganized, bounded, and checked. No normalized extraction was emitted as training prose.
reviewed_regions: all substantive main content on the three required treework pages
all substantive main content on the directly relevant HSE work-at-height and thorough-examination pages
the INDG367 HTML landing page with its linked PDF excluded
the LOLER hub at routing-disposition level
the HSE copyright page at rights-disposition level
all main-content and related links at access and disposition level
logos, shared SVG, images, video, multimedia, PDFs, third-party resources, and legal links at component-disposition level

evidence_and_scope_audit.jurisdiction_gate: Current HSE summaries in United Kingdom occupational arboriculture and work-at-height context; not a universal legal statement or recreational suspension standard.
retained_system_roles: employer or contractor planning and supervision
competent and sufficiently impartial thorough examiner
retained_system_boundary: a person is included in the load
supporting, fixing, and anchoring attachments belong inside the lifting-system boundary
tree anchor points used for rope access, positioning, or rigging form part of the lifting equipment in the HSE page's framework
species, age, condition, and disease influence professional tree-anchor judgment
component markings do not rate an unmarked tree or complete system
retained_line_and_anchor_concepts: two load-bearing tree anchors where possible in the described treework arrangement
distinct separately anchored working and safety lines in rope-access framing
formal risk-assessed exception rather than convenience
supplementary anchors as swing-control geometry rather than automatic full backups
common-mode failures can defeat apparent redundancy
retained_ground_and_change_controls: never wrapping a working rope around the body for grip
continual assessment, plan modification, stop and reassess when unsure
retained_assurance_layers: competent daily check of main climbing rope and associated equipment
written weekly high-wear rope inspection record in HSE arboriculture context
competent systematic thorough examination and written report
excluded_exact_or_operational_material: safe working load and marking rules
statutory examination intervals and retention periods
specific machines, connectors, knots, descenders, fall-arrest devices, and brands
anchor-selection, climbing, changeover, ascent, descent, hauling, lowering, friction, cutting, and rescue steps
legal conclusions beyond the current HSE page text

cross_domain_boundary.source_domain: UK occupational arboriculture and work at height
forbidden_transfers: presenting HSE treework procedure as a bondage standard
treating two anchors or two lines as sufficient design without capacity and common-mode analysis
treating a supplementary anchor as automatically independent or full strength
deriving capacity from species, age, appearance, health, condition, or disease absence
copying treework climbing, rescue, or equipment configuration into recreational suspension
using this digest as UK legal advice outside page scope
cross_domain_boundary.controlling_rule: The corpus provides systems reasoning only. Domain-specific competent assessment, current governing standards, manufacturer information, and the actual load path control; no load-bearing approval is supplied.

retained_scope: species, age, condition, disease, and foreseeable-loading context
line and anchor roles with common-mode caveats
daily checks, recorded rope inspection, examination layers, records, and withdrawal
excluded_material: raw HTML, normalized extraction, site chrome, logos, SVG, media, and linked works
operational climbing, construction, rigging, cutting, rescue, and equipment instructions
exact ratings, intervals, legal fields, enforcement procedure, device endorsements, and brands
URL, title, date, regulation-number, source-order, and component trivia

split_hygiene.document_disjoint_requirement: Assign all eight reviewed HSE pages and every derived Markdown or QA item to one source-family split before chunking or derivation.
